original: First "bug" in a computer program is discovered by a team of engineers, includingGrace Hopper, in Cambridge, Massachusetts, when a real-life moth is removed with tweezers from a relay and taped into the logbook
Connection found between Mammoth Cave Ridge and Flint cave systems in Kentucky, joining 144 miles of passages - making it the world's longest known cave system (later mapped at 420 miles)
1st successful Test flight of a Saturn V
Orville Wrightmakes the first hour-long airplane flight, lasting 62 minutes and 15 seconds at Fort Myer, Virginia
Henry Thomas, American actor (E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial), born in San Antonio, Texas
Viking 2 Mars probe launches
Dennis Ritchie, American computer scientist (created C programming language, helped developed Unix), born in Bronxville, New York (d. 2011)
Hydrofoil designed byAlexander Graham Bell, his wife Mabel Bell and F.W. Casey Baldwin sets new water speed record of 114 km/h on Bras d'Or, Nova Scotia
World's first whole-eye and face transplant is declared successful for a 47-year-old Arkansas man more than a year after the operation
Conestoga 1, 1st private commercial rocket, makes suborbital flight

Imperial Russian Air Service pilot Lt Pyotr Nesterov (26), performs full aerial loop in his Nieuport IV monoplane over Syretzk Aerodrome near Kiev; initially punished for risking government equipment, he was later lauded for his innovation  [27 August O.S.]
Edward Emerson Barnard at Lick Observatory discovers Amalthea, Jupiter's 5th moon

Soviet Union beats the United States 51-50 in the most controversial game in international basketball history; with the US leading 50-49, the final 3 seconds are replayed 3 times until the Soviets finally win
Scientist reveal evidence of humans earliest milk consumption, 6,000 years ago from the dental plaque of teeth of prehistoric farmers from Britain
San Francisco Bay area blanketed by dark orange skies and smoke due to California wildfires
Egyptian archaeologists announce the discovery of a 3,500-year-old tomb of a goldsmith and his family in Draa Abul-Naga, Egypt

PoetJohn Milton's own copy ofShakespeare's First Folio of 1623 has survived with his annotations according to scholar Jason Scott-Warren in Philadelphia library, could be world's most important modern literary discovery
French aviator Jules Védrines is the first to fly over 100 mph (107 mph/172 kph)
English scientist and astronomerJohn Herscheltakes the first glass plate photograph
Congress officially renames the country as the United States of America (from the United Colonies)
US 2021 summer the hottest on record with average 74 degrees Fahrenheit, overtaking record set in 1936, during the Dust Bowl
Australia experiences its earliest and most severe start to the fire season after fighting dozens of bushfires in Queensland and New South Wales
Vietnam's Phong Chau bridge collapses into the Red River, in the wake of Super Typhoon Yagi, taking 10 cars and two scooters with it
Angela Cartwright, English-American actress (The Sound of Music, Lost in Space), born in Altrincham, England
John Kricfalusi, Canadian animator, creator of "The Ren & Stimpy Show", born in Chicoutimi, Quebec
Vitas Gerulaitis bets his house thatMartina Navratilovacan't beat 100th ranked male tennis player
Alexander Twilight, likely the first African American to graduate from a US college, receives a BA degree from Middlebury College
Mary Stuart, at nine months old, is crownedQueen of Scotsin the central Scottish town of Stirling
Queen Elizabeth IIbecomes Great Britain's longest-reigning monarch at 63 years and seven months, beating the previous record set by her great-great-grandmother,Queen Victoria
At 01:46:40 UTC the time on the Unix clock in milliseconds passes 1 billion since January 1, 1970, which Unix systems recognize as zero-time
Charles Durant, 1st US aeronaut, flies a balloon from Castle Garden, NYC to Perth Amboy, NJ, a dsstance of about 25 miles, covered in 3 hours
1st steam engine arrives in North American colonies
Avi Wigderson, Israeli mathematician and computer scientist (2023 Turing Prize for randomness), born in Haifa, Israel
Akshay Kumar, Indian actor, producer, stuntman and TV personality (Rustom, Baby, Hera Pheri), born in Amritsar, Punjab
Elvis Presley's first appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow"
LA Dodgers future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherSandy Koufaxthrows his 4th career no-hitter and first perfect game in a 1-0 win over the Chicago Cubs at Dodger Stadium
Don Zimmer, hits 4,000th Dodger home run
Tom Bradybecomes first player in NFL history to start 300 regular season games as he guides Tampa Bay Buccaneers to an opening day 31-29 win at home to Dallas Cowboys
Pakistan ends India's run of 6 consecutive Olympic field hockey gold medals with a 1-0 win over their sub-continent rivals at the Rome Games
Soviet runner Lyudmila Bragina with a world record 4:01.38 wins the women's 1,500m gold medal at the Munich Olympics
South African Ferrari driver Jody Scheckter wins the Italian Grand Prix at Monza to clinch his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; first RSA champion
John Curry, British figure skater (Olympic gold, World C'ship gold, European C'ship gold 1976), born in Birmingham, England (d. 1994)
Mikhail Tal, Soviet Latvian World Chess Champion (1960-61), born in Riga, Latvia, USSR (d. 1992)
Elvin Jones, American jazz drummer (John Coltrane's "A Love Supreme"), born in Pontiac, Michigan (d. 2004)
Space shuttle STS 64 (Discovery 20) launches into orbit
Denver Broncos beat Boston Patriots, 13-10 in the American Football League's first game before 21,597 fans at Nickerson Field in Boston
MLB Arizona Diamondbacks' lefthanded pitcher Randy Johnson reaches 300 strikeouts for the fifth consecutive season, extending his major league record
Larry Birdof the Celtics begins an NBA free throw streak of 59
NHL great Gordie Howe retires (for the first time), after 25 seasons with the Detroit Red Wings
Apple Records releasesJohn Lennon's second solo studio album, "Imagine" in US; it tops the charts in US, UK, Australia, and 3 other countries
Paul McCartney& Wings begin their "Wings Over The World" tour in Southampton, England; 65 concerts in Europe, Australia, Canada, and United States, runs through October of 1976
4th place finish in the Italian Grand Prix at Monza is enough to clinchJackie Stewarthis 3rd Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
Feyenoord wins soccer's Club World Cup
Challenger returns to Kennedy Space Center via Sheppard AFB, Texas
Uganda declares independence from Great Britain
US PresidentEisenhowersigns 1st civil rights bill since Reconstruction
The National Traffic and Motor Vehicle Safety Act signed into law by U.S. PresidentLyndon B. Johnson, 1st federal safety standards for vehicles and roads
Japanese in South Korea, Taiwan, China, Indochina surrender to Allies
Allied forces liberate Luxembourg
California is admitted as the thirty-first state of the Union
